questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be uniforms for students in the colleges and India as in the schools?
Arguments I. Yes, this will improve the ambience of the colleges as all the students will be decently dressed.
II. No, college students should not be regimented and they should be left to choose their clothes for coming to college.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should the sale of all the toys made in China be banned in India?
Arguments I. Yes, these are very cheap and hence, will put the local toy manufacturers out of business.
II. No, Indian toys are of much better quality and their cost will not be affected.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be no examination upto Std IX in all the schools in India?
Arguments I. No, students need to go through the process of giving examinations right from young age.
II. Yes, this will help students to think laterally and achieve their creative pursuits.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be only a uniform rate of income tax irrespective of the level of income?
Arguments I. Yes, this will substantially reduce the work of the officials of the income tax department.
II. No, this will reduce government tax collection to a large extent.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be only two political parties in India?
Arguments I. Yes, in many developed countries there are only two political parties.
II. No, Indian electorate is not mature to select between only two political parties.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should the sale of tobacco products be restricted to only a few outlets in each city/town?
Arguments I. Yes, this will substantially reduce consumption of tobacco products.
II. No, those who want to purchase tobacco products should get them at convenient locations.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Keeping in consideration the longevity of life in India, should the age-limit for retirement in the government jobs be increased?
Arguments I. Yes, other countries have decided so long before.
II. Yes, it is the actual demand of lakh of employees.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should new universities be established in India?
Arguments I. No, we have not yet achieved the target of literacy.
II. No, we have to face the problems of highly educated but unemployed youths.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should small states be formed out of bigger states in India?
Arguments I. Yes, there will be greater administrative convenience.
II. No, it will jeopardise the national integration.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should all the government owned educational institutions be given to private sector?
Arguments I. Yes, there will be upgradation of educational standard in these institutions.
II. Yes, the educational standard of these institutions will decrease.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should the organisations like the UNO be abolished?
Arguments I. The cold war is going to an end, so there is no role of such organisations.
II. No, in the absence of these organisations, there will be a World War.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should non-vegetarian food be totally banned in our Country?
Arguments I. Yes, it is expensive and therefore, it is beyond the means of most people in our Country.
II. No, nothing should be banned in a democratic Country like ours.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should we impart sex education in schools?
Arguments I. Yes, all the progressive nations do so.
II. No, we cannot impart it in a coeducational school.
